title: 10X-CITEseq protocol (COVID-19 patient samples +/- tetramer stain)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"ci4hr","text":"Purpose","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[{"style":"bold","offset":0,"length":7}],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"8dlpb","text":"To detail the workflow for scCITE-Seq of 24 PBMC samples with 2 technicians. This is based on internal 10X CITEseq protocol v9.","type":"align-left","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: Take two TotalSeq-C Human Panel vials from 4 °C fridge. Briefly centrifuge each tube before opening.
Reconstitute each vial in 50 µL, cell staining buffer .
Vortex for 00:00:05 - 00:00:10 or until the solution visually looks resuspended.
Centrifuge at 10000 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Combine antibody pool from two vials and keep at 4 °C until ready for staining.
Turn water bath on to 37 °C , pre-warm 500 mL, cRPMI media .
While cRPMI warms:
Get a bucket with dry ice and a large container with regular ice.
Label 24 of each of the following tube/vials: a. 15 mL falcon tubes b. Vicell sample tubes
Bring all the vials from LN 2 tank and keep on dry ice until needed.
Aliquot 5 mL, warm cRPMI media into each 15mL tube.
Thaw four vials at a time in 37 °C water bath for 00:01:00 until only a small ice crystal remains and immediately hand over to partner to unscrew the vial aseptically.
Aliquot 100 µL - 125 µL (5-6x10 5 cells) of thawed cells to its respective 15mL falcon tube drop by drop and mix gently twice with a P1000 pipette.
Centrifuge at 300 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes . During this centrifugation step, add 570µL of dPBS to each ViCell sample tube in preparation.
Cap the cryovial and transfer to a Mr. Frosty and move to -80°C freezer for 24:00:00 before moving to LN 2 .
Add 570 µL, dPBS to each ViCell sample tube if not performed previously (step 12).
Aspirate supernatant and resuspend in 200 µL, cRPMI media , keep cells On ice from this step.

Count cells on Vicell ( 30 µL, sample diluted in 570 µL, PBS ). Record total cell count and viability, using template below: i. If the total cell count is above 1x10 6 , the remainder of the cells can be saved for bulk RNA-seq ii. In ViCell counting, use cell-type PBMC and dilution factor 1:20

Calculate volume needed to collect total 1.5 x 10 6 cells from all samples using the template below: i. Calculate volume of each sample needed to get equal number of cells from each sample ii. For samples that don’t have enough cells, thaw more cells from the same vial in -80°C freezer iii. Make note of cell viability and also include dead cells in the total cell count. Do not use samples that have low cell viability (< 85%), or enrich live cell with dead cell removal kit
Take remaining cells from each sample after multiplexing.
Spin at 300 x g, Room temperature, 5 minutes .
Aspirate supernatant.
Either freeze the pellets in the -80°C or proceed to RNA extraction.
Perform RNA Extraction as according to manufacturer’s protocol: Extract RNA with Qiagen RNeasy Plus Mini Kit (elute in 30µL) and quantify RNA with Qubit (Use 1µL for Qubit concentration).
Use SmartSeq-2 protocol for bulk RNA library preparation.
Use cell counts from ViCell to pool each sample into 5mL round bottom polystyrene tubes to get 1.5 x 10 6 cells in total.
Centrifuge at 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Aspirate supernatant, taking care not to disturb the cell pellet
Resuspend cells in 67.5 µL, staining buffer
Incubate for 00:10:00 at 4 °C .
Add 75 µL, antibody pool to pooled sample in FcBlock/staining buffer.
Incubate for 00:45:00 On ice (30-60 min recommended).
Add 2 mL, staining buffer .
Centrifuge at 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Carefully remove supernatant and save in 15mL falcon tube, taking care not to disturb the pellet.
Use wide-bore pipette tip to resuspend cell pellet in 2 mL, staining buffer .
Centrifuge at 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Carefully remove supernatant and save in the same 15mL falcon tube.
Repeat (step 35-37) for one more wash, saving the supernatant:
Use wide-bore pipette tip to resuspend cell pellet in 2 mL, staining buffer .
Centrifuge at 400 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes .
Carefully remove supernatant and save in the same 15mL falcon tube.

Resuspend pellet in a small volume of cRPMI (ie. 100 µL, cRPMI ; if the concentration is too high, cells can be diluted), then pass through a Flowmi strainer. If using Flowmi strainer, be careful not to push the tip too hard as it is fragile. Note that ~ 30% of the cell suspension is lost when using a flowmi strainer. If final volume is very small (less than 50 µL ), use conventional cell strainers

Take 6 µL, filtered cell suspension + 6 µL, trypan blue for counting with Countess and check for count and viability. ( Vicell can be used here instead ). a. The desired cell density is 2.5 x 10 6 cell/mL (or 2,500 cell/µ). b. If cells are too dense, use staining buffer to dilute cells to final density. c. Use 30.8 µL (or appropriate amount if concentration different) for each well of 10x library preparation to ensure 77,000 cells from each sample are added to the master mix . d. Note in 10X VDJ protocol, only 90µL of 100µL cell suspension+master mix is loaded to each well, so the actual cells loaded are 2,500 cell/µL*28µL*(90/100) = 50,000 . e. Maximum volume of cell suspension can be loaded to 10x VDJ run is 31.7 µL ,make up extra volume using staining buffer.
Load 70,000 cells/well into 6 wells of VDJ kits and perform GEM-RT.
Save GEM-RT at 4 °C for up to 3 days or -20 °C for up to 1 week before 10X library preparation
Add 7.5 µL, Fc block reagent
Resuspend cells in 66 µL, staining buffer
Add 4.5 µL, negative control streptavidin with biotinylated BSA (#C0594) 0.05 µg, streptavidin
Incubate 00:15:00 On ice
Add 4.5 µL, S1 protein conjugated streptavidin (#C0591) 0.05 µg, streptavidin
Incubate 00:10:00 On ice
Add 7.5 µL, Fc block reagent
Incubate 00:05:00 On ice
Add the antibody pool ( 75 µL ) to pooled sample
Incubate 00:45:00 On ice 30-60 min recommended
Add 2 mL, staining buffer
Centrifuge 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Carefully remove supernatant and save in 15mL falcon tube, taking care not to disturb the pellet
Use wide-bore pipette tip to resuspend cell pellet in 2 mL, staining buffer
Centrifuge 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Carefully remove supernatant and save in the same 15mL falcon tube
Repeat steps 39-41 for one more wash, saving the supernatant
Use wide-bore pipette tip to resuspend cell pellet in 2 mL, staining buffer
Centrifuge 300 x g, 4 °C, 5 minutes
Carefully remove supernatant and save in the same 15mL falcon tube

Resuspend pellet in a small volume of resuspension buffer (i.e. 100 μL; if the concentration is too high, cells can be diluted) and pass through a Flowmi strainer If using Flowmi strainer, be vareful not to push the tip too hard as it is fragile ~30% of the cell suspension is lost when using a Flowmi strainer If final volume is very small (less than 50 μL) use conventional cell strainers

Take 6 µL, filtered cell suspension and 6 µL, Trypan blue for counting with Countess Vicell can be used here instead The desired cell density is 2.5x10 6 cell/mL (or 2,500 cell/μL) If cells are too dense, use staining buffer to dilute cells to final density Use 30.8 μL (or appropriate amount if concentration is different) for each well of 10x library preparation to ensure 77,000 cells from each sample are added to the mastermix Note in 10X V(D)J protocol, only 90 μL of the 100 μL cell suspension + mastermix is loaded to each well, so the actual cells loaded are 2,000 cell/μL x 30.8 μL x (90/100) = 70,000 Maximum volume of cell suspension can be loaded to 10X V(D)J run is 31.7 μL, make up extra volume using staining buffer
Load 70,000 cells each well into 6 wells and perform GEM-RT
GEM-RT can be stored at 4 °C for up to 72:00:00 or at -20 °C for up to 168:00:00
creator: Alyssa Ward
